prompt: Invent an original, vivid metaphor for the experience of learning a new language as an adult. Explain it in three sentences.
response: Learning a new language as an adult is like assembling a mosaic in a dimly lit room—your hands fumble with unfamiliar tiles (words and grammar), and the full picture remains hidden until enough pieces click into place. At first, every fragment feels alien and misshapen, resisting your clumsy attempts to fit it into the emerging pattern. But with patience and persistence, the image slowly sharpens, revealing not just a new way to communicate, but a new way to see the world through someone else’s eyes.
